name: Kenny Yeboah
headline: Yeboah's Cardinals Audition Starts From The Bottom
body:
Yeboah has barely cracked the field across his career, logging just 19% of snaps over seven games in 2024 and running only 41 routes. The flashes exist when he plays: a 0.34 EPA per play, a 50% success rate, and 1.15 yards per route run all hint at a competent role player when targeted. But the sample is microscopic, anchored by a 1.4% target share, five receptions, 47 yards, and one touchdown. A 25% drop rate on his limited looks is the glaring red flag, and his 0.195 targets per route run can't be trusted at that volume.

Now in Arizona, he arrives as a tryout signing fighting for a reserve job in a crowded tight end room. There's no defined path to snaps, no ADP, and effectively zero roster relevance. Treat him as a deep-league dart throw only if camp news points toward a real special-teams or blocking role.
